Class Assassin

java.lang.Object
core.Piece
pieces.Assassin
All Implemented Interfaces:
Moveable, Stabbable
public class Assassin extends Piece implements Stabbable
Represents an Assassin piece within the Kuroshiro engine. The Assassin is a highly mobile melee unit that moves and attacks diagonally up to 2 squares away. Similar to a standard chess Knight, its movement is a "leap", meaning it completely ignores any intervening pieces along its path and captures by landing directly on an enemy square.

  • Field Summary

  • Constructor Details

    • Assassin

      public Assassin(Color color, Position position)
      Initializes a new Assassin piece.
      Parameters:
      color - the Color of the Assassin
      position - the initial starting Position on the board

  • Method Details

    • getLegalMoves

      public List<Position> getLegalMoves(Board board)
      Calculates all legal movement and attack destinations for the Assassin. The Assassin evaluates squares along a diagonal path up to its maximum jump range. Because it leaps, it does not check for line-of-sight blockages. A landing square is valid if it remains on the board and is either empty or occupied by an enemy.
      Specified by:
      getLegalMoves in interface Moveable
      Specified by:
      getLegalMoves in class Piece
      Parameters:
      board - the current state of the game Board
      Returns:
      a list of valid Position coordinates the Assassin can land on

    • stab

      public Piece stab(Position target, Board board)
      Executes a melee attack on a designated target position. Because the Assassin implements Stabbable, this method handles the immediate removal of the captured piece. The engine will handle moving the Assassin to this landing square afterward.
      Specified by:
      stab in interface Stabbable
      Parameters:
      target - the Position being attacked and landed on
      board - the current state of the game Board
      Returns:
      the captured Piece that was removed from the board, or null if empty

    • getSymbol

      public String getSymbol()
      Returns the unique symbol used to represent the Assassin in text-based rendering.
      Specified by:
      getSymbol in interface Moveable
      Specified by:
      getSymbol in class Piece
      Returns:
      the string "S"
